name: Affordable Housing Trust for Columbus and Franklin County
description_short: Nonprofit CDFI lender providing construction and acquisition loans for affordable housing development in Columbus and Franklin County since 2001.
description_long:
The Affordable Housing Trust for Columbus and Franklin County (AHT) was established in 2001 as an independent, nonprofit lender focused on expanding affordable housing opportunities in Central Ohio. In February 2016, AHT achieved Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) certification, positioning it as a specialized lending entity dedicated to underserved markets. Over 20+ years, the organization has worked with the City of Columbus and Franklin County as primary funding partners to serve thousands of low and moderate-income families.

AHT provides construction, acquisition, and rehabilitation loans primarily to community development corporations, nonprofit builders, and for-profit developers—not directly to individual homebuyers. Their lending supports affordable rental housing, supportive housing, and homeownership projects. In 2024 alone, AHT closed $70 million in loans, facilitated development of 1,900+ units, and generated $494 million in Central Ohio economic impact. The organization has successfully funded 300+ projects since inception.

AHT distinguishes itself through sector-spanning partnerships (private, nonprofit, and public), CDFI certification enabling access to specialized funding sources, and a documented commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. Their structured lending process includes consultation, application, board approval, and closing phases. The organization intentionally partners with developers serving marginalized and underrepresented communities while strengthening neighborhood stability.

However, AHT is a developer-focused lender, not a consumer-facing mortgage provider. Individuals seeking affordable rental housing are directed to the Ohio Housing Locator, while those needing Section 8 vouchers or public housing should contact Columbus Metropolitan Housing Authority. This is institutional lending for housing professionals, not retail mortgage lending.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: CDFI-certified lender with specialized expertise in affordable housing financing
20+ years of established track record with 300+ successfully funded projects
$520+ million in total loans closed demonstrates proven lending capacity
Partnerships with City of Columbus and Franklin County as primary funding sources provide stability
Multi-sector approach works with private, nonprofit, and public developers
Documented commitment to equity and inclusion in underserved communities
Generates measurable economic impact ($494M in 2024 alone)
cons: Not a consumer retail lender—does not provide loans directly to individuals for home purchase or refinance
Limited to Columbus and Franklin County geographic area
Directs individual housing applicants elsewhere, indicating no direct consumer mortgage products
Requires developer/builder status to apply; residential borrowers must work through partner organizations
best_for: Community development corporations and nonprofit developers building affordable housing
For-profit builders developing affordable rental or homeownership projects
Local governments and housing authorities partnering on affordable housing initiatives
diagnosis: AHT is a specialized nonprofit lender for affordable housing developers and community organizations, not individual borrowers. Best suited for nonprofits, CDCs, and builders developing affordable housing in Central Ohio seeking institutional construction and acquisition financing with mission-aligned terms.
services: Construction loans for new affordable housing development
Acquisition loans for existing property rehabilitation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
